exact claim text: Qwen clean payload recovery is stable across the frozen payload-by-seed matrix
quantitative result: G1: 48/48 included clean cases; accepted/verifier/decoded_payload rates all 1.0; 95% CI low 0.9259; 0 pending; 0 excluded
required caveat: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct only; clean exact-slot compiled protocol; not a robustness or cross-model claim
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim broad model-family robustness or superiority over Perinucleus from this result
missing evidence: Matched FAR and utility comparison remain separate evidence

exact claim text: Prompt-family stability holds for the tested equivalent prompt families
quantitative result: G2: 36/36 included cases across PF1/PF2/PF3 x U00/U03/U12/U15 x seeds 17/23/29; accepted/verifier/decoded rates 1.0
required caveat: Only three pre-registered semantically equivalent prompt families; not arbitrary prompt rewriting
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim prompt-invariant ownership under unrestricted prompt transformations
missing evidence: Broader prompt distribution and adversarial prompt wrappers

exact claim text: Block-count scaling is strong but not perfect under the held-out G3a-v3 matrix
quantitative result: G3a-v3: 142/144 valid successes; 2 valid method failures; exact/RS success rate 0.9861; B1 48/48, B2 46/48, B4 48/48; slot_bucket_accuracy mean 0.9948
required caveat: Report the two valid method failures in the denominator; this is block-count scaling, not bucket-member scaling
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim 100% scaling or failure-free behavior across all block counts
missing evidence: Larger block counts and separate bucket-member scale (G3b) if that claim is needed

exact claim text: Training-signal scaling remains successful over the tested sample-count ladder
quantitative result: G4: 48/48 valid successes across S16/S32/S64/S128; exact/RS/slot_bucket_accuracy all 1.0; 0 method failures; 0 pending
required caveat: Only effective compiled sample count varies; same Qwen model, B2, prompt family, codebook, and G3a-v3 hp04 objective; S128 repeats 64 unique samples twice
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim universal data efficiency or generalization beyond the frozen ladder
missing evidence: Independent larger unique-sample ladders and additional models

exact claim text: Our method has higher clean ownership success than official Scalable/Perinucleus
quantitative result: Not supported: ours G1 is 48/48 and official Qwen-adapted Perinucleus is also 48/48
required caveat: The correct statement is clean success parity, not superiority
forbidden overclaim: Do not write that ours outperforms Perinucleus on clean exact verification
missing evidence: A differentiating matched metric such as FAR, utility, compute, persistence, or payload capacity

exact claim text: Clean ownership success is tied with the official Qwen-adapted Scalable/Perinucleus baseline
quantitative result: Ours: 48/48, success_rate 1.0; official Qwen-adapted Perinucleus: 48/48, success_rate 1.0; both have 0 method failures and 0 pending
required caveat: Perinucleus row must be labeled Qwen-adapted official Scalable/Perinucleus; parity is clean exact-verifier parity only
forbidden overclaim: Do not imply the methods have the same mechanism or that parity establishes our superiority
missing evidence: Matched FAR, utility, compute, and robustness/persistence comparisons

exact claim text: The ownership decision is FAR-calibrated against null models for the main method and Perinucleus
quantitative result: Only internal fixed/uniform baseline calibration is frozen at target_far 0.01 with observed FAR 0.0 on available negative sets; no matched FAR artifact for ours vs official Perinucleus
required caveat: A calibration protocol exists, but the main method and official Perinucleus do not yet have a matched FAR/null comparison artifact
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim FAR-calibrated ownership for the main Perinucleus comparison
missing evidence: Null calibration for ours and Perinucleus over base Qwen, wrong payload/owner probes, random organic prompts, and fixed query budgets M=1/3/5/10

exact claim text: The method preserves utility better than Perinucleus
quantitative result: Perinucleus candidate utility sanity passed: adapter utility 0.6192 vs base 0.6035 on tinyBenchmarks; signed absolute_drop -0.01565; no matched utility result for ours on the same benchmark
required caveat: Can cite Perinucleus utility sanity only as baseline validation; cannot compare utility preservation without ours on the same suite
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim better utility, lower utility drop, or no degradation versus Perinucleus
missing evidence: Same utility benchmark for base, ours, and Perinucleus with confidence intervals

exact claim text: Our method is compute-efficient relative to official Scalable/Perinucleus
quantitative result: Compute accounting exists but is not normalized for a matched ours-vs-Perinucleus efficiency comparison; examples: G1 requested 864 train + 864 eval GPU-hours; Perinucleus final eval seconds 732.1 and 228 model forwards
required caveat: Can report compute accounting descriptively; cannot claim relative efficiency without matched wall-clock/GPU-hour accounting for both methods
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim lower cost, better efficiency, or cheaper verification versus Perinucleus
missing evidence: Matched train wall-clock, GPU-hours, eval query cost, trainable parameters, and artifact size for both methods

exact claim text: The method achieves exact cross-family replication on Llama 3.1 8B
quantitative result: R1 exact gate: 0/12; valid method failures: 12/12; claim_paper_ready false
required caveat: The exact cross-family result is negative; it can be discussed only as a failed exact-surface replication
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim exact Llama replication or cross-family exact success
missing evidence: A pre-registered R1-v2 exact repair would be required for this claim

exact claim text: R1 supports cross-family RS-aware semantic recovery
quantitative result: R1 RS-aware gate: 12/12; slot_bucket_accuracy mean 1.0; symbol_error_count mean 0.0; exact gate 0/12
required caveat: State as diagnostic RS-aware/bucket semantic recovery, not exact representative replication; small matrix on Llama 3.1 8B Instruct
forbidden overclaim: Do not collapse RS-aware recovery into exact clean-path replication
missing evidence: Additional model families and larger payload matrices if claiming broad cross-family transfer

exact claim text: The verifier is robust to the tested whitespace perturbation
quantitative result: Batch3 whitespace_scrub: accepted_before 4/4 and accepted_after 4/4; accepted_after_rate 1.0
required caveat: Only the tested whitespace_scrub attack on accepted clean baselines; not broad post-processing robustness
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim robustness to arbitrary formatting, truncation, delimiter removal, paraphrase, or output-side transformations
missing evidence: Larger attack grid and adversarial post-processing suite

exact claim text: The verifier is robust to truncation or delimiter destruction
quantitative result: Not supported as a positive claim: truncate_tail accepted_after 0/4; delimiter_scrub accepted_after 0/4; accepted_before 4/4 for both
required caveat: The supported statement is the opposite: truncation and delimiter scrubbing break current acceptance and are outside the current guarantee
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim broad output-side robustness or robustness to structure-destroying transformations
missing evidence: A revised robust verifier and a new accepted-before/accepted-after attack package

exact claim text: Bucket-mass objective supports bucket-level semantic recovery while exact representative recovery is diagnostic
quantitative result: T2-r1: bucket_mass bucket_correct_rate 1.0 but slot_exact_rate 0.5; fixed_representative exact-slot pass; uniform_bucket bucket_correct_rate 1.0 but slot_exact_rate 0.0
required caveat: Single U15 seed-17 multi-member-bucket diagnostic; supports metric hierarchy, not a universal objective ranking
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim bucket_mass always beats fixed representative or guarantees exact canonical tokens
missing evidence: Broader multi-member bucket matrix and utility/lexical distortion metrics

exact claim text: Compiled evidence is next-token measurable under the exact-slot verifier
quantitative result: T1: contextual_exact accepted with slot_exact_rate 1.0 and verifier_success true; repaired sequence_proxy accepted with slot_exact_rate 1.0 and verifier_success true for U03 seed 17
required caveat: Empirical support for the implementation path; formal theorem text must carry the mathematical claim; sequence_proxy required payload-label repair
forbidden overclaim: Do not claim all textual evidence is next-token measurable or that arbitrary generation is verifier-compatible
